name: KrillApp.Server
title: Krill Server
description: Represents a Krill server instance running on a Raspberry Pi or Debian-based Linux system. Manages data points, executes automation, and handles device communication. When local, starts a network beacon to advertise itself. When remote, connects to the server via HTTPS. Contains child nodes for GPIO pins, peer connections, LLM integration, and serial devices.
shortDescription: A Krill server instance that manages devices, automation, and data on a Raspberry Pi or Linux host.
llmPurpose: Represents a Krill server instance that manages GPIO pins, serial devices, peer connections, LLM integration, and automation execution.
llmBehavior: advertises server on network via beacon (if local)
connects to remote server via HTTPS (if remote)
contains and manages child hardware and service nodes
llmInputs: server hostname, port, API key, platform type
llmTypicalUseCases: represent a Raspberry Pi running Krill
connect to a remote Krill server
organize GPIO pins and serial devices under a server
llmCreationHints: A Server node is typically auto-discovered via beacon or manually added by the user. It is the root container for all hardware and service nodes on that host.

llmConnectionHints.role: root structural node for a physical or remote server

llmExamples: Connect to the Raspberry Pi on my network.
Show the GPIO pins on this server.
Add a new Krill server at 192.168.1.10.
